title: Alluvial Plotting for Single-Cell Object — alluvialClones
code: alluvialClones(   sc.data,   clone.call = NULL,   chain = "both",   y.axes = NULL,   color = NULL,   facet = NULL,   alpha = NULL,   top.clones = NULL,   min.freq = 0,   highlight.clones = NULL,   highlight.color = "red",   stratum.width = 0.2,   flow.alpha = 0.5,   show.labels = TRUE,   label.size = 2,   order.strata = NULL,   export.table = NULL,   palette = "inferno",   cloneCall = NULL,   exportTable = NULL,   ... )

title: Alluvial Plotting for Single-Cell Object — alluvialClones
text: sc.data product combineExpression(). clone.call Defines clonal sequence grouping. Accepted values : gene (VDJC genes), nt (CDR3 nucleotide sequence), aa (CDR3 amino acid sequence), strict (VDJC + nt). custom column header can also used. chain TCR/BCR chain use. Use include chains (e.g., TRA/TRB). Accepted values: TRA, TRB, TRG, TRD, IGH, IGL, IGK, Light (light chains), (TRA/B Heavy/Light). y.axes columns separate proportional visualizations. color column header clone(s) highlighted. facet column label separate. alpha column header gradated opacity. top.clones Show top N clones frequency. NULL (default), show clones. min.freq Minimum frequency threshold displaying flows. Clones appearing fewer many times filtered . highlight.clones Character vector specific clone sequences highlight. clones colored distinctly others shown gray. highlight.color Color use highlighted clones (default: "red"). stratum.width Width stratum bars (default: 0.2). flow.alpha Transparency flows (default: 0.5). Highlighted clones use full opacity. show.labels TRUE (default), display stratum labels. label.size Text size stratum labels (default: 2). order.strata Named list specifying order levels within stratum. Names match column names y.axes. export.table TRUE, returns data frame results instead plot. palette Colors use visualization - input hcl.pals. cloneCall Use clone.call instead. exportTable Use export.table instead. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot theme

title: Alluvial Plotting for Single-Cell Object — alluvialClones
text: ggplot object visualizing categorical distribution clones, data.frame export.table = TRUE.

title: Alluvial Plotting for Single-Cell Object — alluvialClones
code: # Getting the combined contigs combined <- combineTCR(contig_list,                         samples = c("P17B", "P17L", "P18B", "P18L",                                     "P19B","P19L", "P20B", "P20L"))  # Getting a sample of a Seurat object scRep_example <- get(data("scRep_example"))  # Using combineExpresion() scRep_example <- combineExpression(combined, scRep_example) scRep_example$Patient <- substring(scRep_example$orig.ident, 1,3)  # Using alluvialClones() alluvialClones(scRep_example,                    clone.call = "gene",                    y.axes = c("Patient", "ident"),                    color = "ident")   # Show only top 50 most frequent clones alluvialClones(scRep_example,                    clone.call = "aa",                    y.axes = c("Patient", "ident"),                    top.clones = 50)   # Highlight specific clones alluvialClones(scRep_example,                    clone.call = "aa",                    y.axes = c("Patient", "ident"),                    highlight.clones = c("CVVSDNTGGFKTIF_CASSVRRERANTGELFF")) #> Warning: Use of `lodes[[".highlight"]]` is discouraged. #> ℹ Use `.data[[".highlight"]]` instead. #> Warning: Use of `lodes[[".highlight"]]` is discouraged. #> ℹ Use `.data[[".highlight"]]` instead.

what: Annotate invariant T cells (MAIT or iNKT) in single-cell TCR data — annotateInvariant
title: Annotate invariant T cells (MAIT or iNKT) in single-cell TCR data — annotateInvariant
text: annotateInvariant() function identifies potential mucosal-associated invariant T (MAIT``) cells invariant natural killer T (iNKT`) cells single-cell sequencing datasets based characteristic  TCR usage. extracts TCR chain information provided single-cell data, checks known invariant T-cell receptor criteria either MAIT iNKT cells, returns score indicating presence (1) absence (0) invariant cell populations individual cell. function supports data mouse human samples, providing convenient method annotate specialized T-cell subsets within single-cell analyses.

title: Annotate invariant T cells (MAIT or iNKT) in single-cell TCR data — annotateInvariant

title: Annotate invariant T cells (MAIT or iNKT) in single-cell TCR data — annotateInvariant

what: Cluster clones by sequence similarity — clonalCluster
title: Cluster clones by sequence similarity — clonalCluster
text: function clusters TCRs BCRs based edit distance alignment score CDR3 sequences. can operate either nucleotide (nt) amino acid (aa) sequences can optionally enforce clones share V /J genes. output can input object added metadata column cluster IDs, sparse adjacency matrix, igraph graph object representing cluster network.

title: Cluster clones by sequence similarity — clonalCluster
code: clonalCluster(   input.data,   chain = "TRB",   sequence = "aa",   threshold = 0.85,   group.by = NULL,   dist.type = NULL,   dist.mat = NULL,   normalize = "length",   gap.open = NULL,   gap.extend = NULL,   cluster.method = "components",   cluster.prefix = "cluster.",   use.V = TRUE,   use.J = FALSE,   export.adj.matrix = NULL,   export.graph = NULL,   dist_type = NULL,   dist_mat = NULL,   gap_open = NULL,   gap_extend = NULL,   exportAdjMatrix = NULL,   exportGraph = NULL )

title: Cluster clones by sequence similarity — clonalCluster
text: input.data product combineTCR(), combineBCR() combineExpression(). chain TCR/BCR chain use. Use include chains (e.g., TRA/TRB). Accepted values: TRA, TRB, TRG, TRD, IGH, IGL, IGK, Light (light chains), (TRA/B Heavy/Light). sequence Clustering based either aa nt sequences. threshold similarity threshold. < 1, treated normalized similarity (higher stricter). >= 1, treated raw edit distance (lower stricter). group.column header metadata lists group analysis (e.g., "sample", "treatment"). NULL, clusters calculated across sequences. dist.type distance metric use. Options: "levenshtein" (default), "hamming", "damerau" (allows transpositions), "nw" (Needleman-Wunsch), "sw" (Smith-Waterman). dist.mat substitution matrix use alignment-based metrics ("nw" "sw"). Options: "BLOSUM45", "BLOSUM50", "BLOSUM62", "BLOSUM80" (default), "BLOSUM100", "PAM30", "PAM40", "PAM70", "PAM120", "PAM250", "identity". normalize Method normalizing distances. Options: "none", "maxlen" (divide max sequence length), "length" (default, divide mean sequence length). threshold < 1, controls similarity calculated. gap.open Penalty opening gap alignment metrics (default: -10). gap.extend Penalty extending gap alignment metrics (default: -1). cluster.method clustering algorithm use. Defaults "components", finds connected subgraphs. cluster.prefix character prefix add cluster names (e.g., "cluster."). use.V TRUE, sequences must share V gene clustered together. use.J TRUE, sequences must share J gene clustered together. export.adj.matrix TRUE, function returns sparse adjacency matrix (dgCMatrix) network. export.graph TRUE, function returns igraph object sequence network. dist_type Use dist.type instead. dist_mat Use dist.mat instead. gap_open Use gap.open instead. gap_extend Use gap.extend instead. exportAdjMatrix Use export.adj.matrix instead. exportGraph Use export.graph instead.

title: Cluster clones by sequence similarity — clonalCluster
text: Depending export parameters, one following: amended input.data object new metadata column containing cluster IDs (default). igraph object export.graph = TRUE. sparse dgCMatrix object export.adj.matrix = TRUE.

title: Cluster clones by sequence similarity — clonalCluster
text: clustering process follows: function retrieves relevant chain data input object. calculates distance sequences within group (across entire dataset group.NULL). edge list constructed, connecting sequences meet similarity threshold. threshold parameter behaves differently based value: threshold < 1 (e.g., 0.85): Interpreted normalized distance. higher value means greater similarity required. threshold >= 1 (e.g., 2): Interpreted maximum raw edit distance. lower value means greater similarity required. Distance Metrics: Levenshtein/Hamming/Damerau: Standard edit distance calculations. Alignment (NW/SW): dist.type "nw" (Needleman-Wunsch) "sw" (Smith-Waterman), alignment scores calculated using specified substitution matrix (dist.mat). scores converted distance-like metric clustering. igraph graph built edge list. clustering algorithm run graph (default: connected components).

title: Cluster clones by sequence similarity — clonalCluster
code: # Getting the combined contigs combined <- combineTCR(contig_list,                        samples = c("P17B", "P17L", "P18B", "P18L",                                    "P19B","P19L", "P20B", "P20L"))  # Standard Levenshtein clustering (85% similarity) sub_combined <- clonalCluster(combined[c(1,2)],                               chain = "TRA",                               sequence = "aa",                               threshold = 0.85)  # Alignment-based clustering using BLOSUM80 sub_combined_nw <- clonalCluster(combined[c(1,2)],                                  chain = "TRA",                                  dist.type = "nw",                                  dist.mat = "BLOSUM80",                                  threshold = 0.85)  # Export the graph object instead graph_obj <- clonalCluster(combined[c(1,2)],                            chain = "TRA",                            export.graph = TRUE)

title: Calculate Clonal Diversity — clonalDiversity
code: clonalDiversity(   input.data,   clone.call = NULL,   metric = "shannon",   chain = "both",   group.by = NULL,   order.by = NULL,   x.axis = NULL,   export.table = NULL,   palette = "inferno",   n.boots = 100,   return.boots = FALSE,   skip.boots = FALSE,   cloneCall = NULL,   exportTable = NULL,   ... )

title: Calculate Clonal Diversity — clonalDiversity
text: input.data product combineTCR(), combineBCR(), combineExpression(). clone.call Defines clonal sequence grouping. Accepted values : gene (VDJC genes), nt (CDR3 nucleotide sequence), aa (CDR3 amino acid sequence), strict (VDJC + nt). custom column header can also used. metric diversity metric calculate. Must single string list available metrics (see Details). chain TCR/BCR chain use. Use include chains (e.g., TRA/TRB). Accepted values: TRA, TRB, TRG, TRD, IGH, IGL, IGK, Light (light chains), (TRA/B Heavy/Light). group.column header metadata lists group analysis (e.g., "sample", "treatment"). NULL, data analyzed list element active identity case single-cell objects. order.character vector defining desired order elements group.variable. Alternatively, use alphanumeric sort groups automatically. x.axis additional metadata variable group samples along x-axis. export.table TRUE, returns data frame matrix results instead plot. palette Colors use visualization - input hcl.pals. n.boots number bootstrap iterations perform (default 100). return.boots TRUE, returns bootstrap values instead mean. Automatically enables export.table. skip.boots TRUE, disables downsampling bootstrapping. metric calculated full dataset group. Defaults FALSE. cloneCall Use clone.call instead. exportTable Use export.table instead. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot theme

title: Calculate Clonal Diversity — clonalDiversity
text: ggplot object visualizing diversity metric, data.frame export.table = TRUE.

title: Calculate Clonal Diversity — clonalDiversity
text: function operates first splitting dataset specified group.variable. Downsampling Bootstrapping: make fair comparison groups different sizes, diversity metrics often require normalization. function implements downsampling. determines number clones smallest group. group, performs n.boots iterations (default = 100). iteration, randomly samples clones (replacement) size smallest group. calculates selected diversity metric downsampled set. final reported diversity value mean results bootstrap iterations. process can disabled setting skip.boots = TRUE. Available Diversity Metrics (metric): function uses registry metrics imported immApex package. can select one following: "shannon": Shannon's Entropy. See shannon_entropy. "inv.simpson": Inverse Simpson Index. See inv_simpson. "gini.simpson": Gini-Simpson Index. See gini_simpson. "norm.entropy": Normalized Shannon Entropy. See norm_entropy. "pielou": Pielou's Evenness (norm.entropy). See pielou_evenness. "ace": Abundance-based Coverage Estimator. See ace_richness. "chao1": Chao1 Richness Estimator. See chao1_richness. "gini": Gini Coefficient inequality. See gini_coef. "d50": number top clones making 50% library. See d50_dom. "hill0", "hill1", "hill2": Hill numbers order 0, 1, 2. See hill_q.

title: Calculate Clonal Diversity — clonalDiversity
text: Andrew Malone, Nick Borcherding, Nathan Vanderkraan

title: Calculate Clonal Diversity — clonalDiversity
code: # Making combined contig data combined <- combineTCR(contig_list,                        samples = c("P17B", "P17L", "P18B", "P18L",                                    "P19B","P19L", "P20B", "P20L"))  # Calculate Shannon diversity, grouped by sample clonalDiversity(combined,                 clone.call = "gene",                 metric = "shannon")   # Calculate Inverse Simpson without bootstrapping clonalDiversity(combined,                 clone.call = "aa",                 metric = "inv.simpson",                 skip.boots = TRUE)

text: input.data product combineTCR(), combineBCR(), combineExpression() clone.call Defines clonal sequence grouping. title: Examining the clonal overlap between groups or samples — clonalOverlap
text: ggplot object visualizing clonal overlap data.frame exportTable = TRUE.

title: Examining the clonal overlap between groups or samples — clonalOverlap
text: formulas indices follows: Overlap Coefficient: $$overlap = \frac{\sum \min(, b)}{\min(\sum , \sum b)}$$ Raw Count Overlap: $$raw = \sum \min(, b)$$ Morisita Index: $$morisita = \frac{\sum b}{(\sum )(\sum b)}$$ Jaccard Index: $$jaccard = \frac{\sum \min(, b)}{\sum + \sum b - \sum \min(, b)}$$ Cosine Similarity: $$cosine = \frac{\sum b}{\sqrt{(\sum ^2)(\sum b^2)}}$$ : \(\) \(b\) abundances species \(\) groups B, respectively.

title: Examining the clonal overlap between groups or samples — clonalOverlap

title: Plot powerTCR Clustering Based on Clonal Size — clonalSizeDistribution
text: probability density function (pdf) Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) given : $$f(x|\mu, \sigma, \xi) = \frac{1}{\sigma} \left( 1 + \xi \left( \frac{x - \mu}{\sigma} \right) \right)^{-\left( \frac{1}{\xi} + 1 \right)}$$ : \(\mu\) location parameter \(\sigma > 0\) scale parameter \(\xi\) shape parameter \(x \ge \mu\) \(\xi \ge 0\) \(\mu \le x \le \mu - \sigma/\xi\) \(\xi < 0\) probability density function (pdf) Gamma Distribution given : $$f(x|\alpha, \beta) = \frac{x^{\alpha-1} e^{-x/\beta}}{\beta^\alpha \Gamma(\alpha)}$$ : \(\alpha > 0\) shape parameter \(\beta > 0\) scale parameter \(x \ge 0\) \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) gamma function \(\alpha\)

what: Combine B Cell Receptor Contig Data — combineBCR
title: Combine B Cell Receptor Contig Data — combineBCR
text: function consolidates list BCR sequencing results level individual cell barcodes. Using samples ID parameters, function add strings prefixes prevent issues repeated barcodes. resulting new barcodes need match Seurat SCE object order use, combineExpression(). Unlike combineTCR(), combineBCR produces column CTstrict based edit distance clustering clonalCluster(). CTstrict column formatted Heavy_Light (underscore-separated) downstream compatibility. Connected clones labeled cluster.X, unconnected clones (singlets) labeled V gene CDR3 sequence (e.g., IGHV3-64.CAKSYS..._IGKV3-15.CQQYSN...).

title: Combine B Cell Receptor Contig Data — combineBCR
code: combineBCR(   input.data,   samples = NULL,   ID = NULL,   chain = "both",   sequence = "nt",   dist.type = NULL,   dist.mat = NULL,   normalize = "length",   gap.open = NULL,   gap.extend = NULL,   call.related.clones = TRUE,   group.by = NULL,   threshold = 0.85,   cluster.method = "components",   use.V = TRUE,   use.J = TRUE,   remove.na = NULL,   remove.multi = NULL,   filter.multi = NULL,   filter.nonproductive = NULL,   removeNA = NULL,   removeMulti = NULL,   filterMulti = NULL,   filterNonproductive = NULL,   dist_type = NULL,   dist_mat = NULL,   gap_open = NULL,   gap_extend = NULL )

title: Combine B Cell Receptor Contig Data — combineBCR
text: input.data List filtered contig annotations outputs loadContigs(). samples character vector sample labels. Must length input list. ID optional character vector additional sample identifiers. chain chain use clustering call.related.clones = TRUE. Passed clonalCluster(). Default "". sequence sequence type ("nt" "aa") use clustering. Passed clonalCluster(). Default "nt". dist.type distance metric use. Options: "levenshtein" (default), "hamming", "damerau", "nw" (Needleman-Wunsch), "sw" (Smith-Waterman). dist.mat substitution matrix use alignment-based metrics ("nw" "sw"). Options include "BLOSUM62", "PAM30", etc. normalize Method normalizing distances. Options: "none" (default), "maxlen", "length". gap.open Penalty opening gap alignment metrics (default: -10). gap.extend Penalty extending gap alignment metrics (default: -1). call.related.clones Logical. TRUE, uses clonalCluster() identify related clones based sequence similarity. FALSE, defines clones exact V-gene CDR3 amino acid sequence. group.column header used group clones. (`NULL“), clusters calculated across samples. threshold similarity threshold passed clonalCluster() call.related.clones = TRUE. See ?clonalCluster details. cluster.method clustering algorithm use. Defaults "components", finds connected subgraphs. use.V Logical. TRUE, sequences must share V gene clustered together. use.J Logical. TRUE, sequences must share J gene clustered together. remove.na remove chain without values. remove.multi Logical. TRUE, removes cells one distinct heavy light chain processing. filter.multi Logical. TRUE, filters multi-chain cells retain abundant IGH IGL/IGK chains. filter.nonproductive Logical. TRUE, removes non-productive contigs analysis. removeNA Use remove.na instead. removeMulti Use remove.multi instead. filterMulti Use filter.multi instead. filterNonproductive Use filter.nonproductive instead. dist_type Use dist.type instead. dist_mat Use dist.mat instead. gap_open Use gap.open instead. gap_extend Use gap.extend instead.

title: Combine B Cell Receptor Contig Data — combineBCR

what: Generate Data Frame to Plot Chord Diagram — getCirclize
title: Generate Data Frame to Plot Chord Diagram — getCirclize
text: function take meta data product combineExpression() generate relational data frame used chord diagram. chord represent number clones unique shared across multiple group.variable. using downstream circlize R package, please read cite following manuscript. looking advanced ways circular visualizations, great cookbook circlize package.

title: Generate Data Frame to Plot Chord Diagram — getCirclize
code: getCirclize(   sc.data,   clone.call = NULL,   group.by = NULL,   method = c("unique", "abundance", "jaccard", "overlap"),   proportion = FALSE,   symmetric = TRUE,   include.self = TRUE,   include.metadata = FALSE,   min.shared = 0,   top.links = NULL,   filter.sectors = NULL,   palette = "inferno",   cloneCall = NULL )

title: Generate Data Frame to Plot Chord Diagram — getCirclize
text: sc.data single-cell object combineExpression(). clone.call Defines clonal sequence grouping. Accepted values : gene (VDJC genes), nt (CDR3 nucleotide sequence), aa (CDR3 amino acid sequence), strict (VDJC + nt). custom column header can also used. group.column header (vector column headers hierarchical grouping) metadata group analysis (e.g., "sample", "treatment"). NULL, data analyzed active identity. multiple columns provided, combined "_" separator multi-level annotations. method method calculating link values: "unique" (default) counts unique shared clones, "abundance" sums clone frequencies, "jaccard" calculates Jaccard similarity, "overlap" calculates overlap coefficient. proportion Calculate relationship unique clones (FALSE, default) normalized proportion (TRUE). symmetric TRUE (default), returns symmetric relationships. FALSE, returns directional flow showing proportion source's clones found destination. include.self Include counting clones within single group.comparison. include.metadata TRUE, returns list links data frame sector-level metadata including cell counts, clone counts, expansion metrics. min.shared Minimum number shared clones include link (default 0). top.links Keep top N links value. NULL (default), keep . filter.sectors Character vector sectors include. NULL, include . palette Colors use sector color suggestions - input hcl.pals. cloneCall Use clone.call instead.

title: Generate Data Frame to Plot Chord Diagram — getCirclize
text: data frame shared clones groups formatted chordDiagram. include.metadata = TRUE, returns list links (edge data frame), sectors (sector-level statistics), colors (suggested colors sector).

title: Generate Data Frame to Plot Chord Diagram — getCirclize
text: Dillon Corvino, Nick Borcherding

title: Generate Data Frame to Plot Chord Diagram — getCirclize
code: # Getting the combined contigs combined <- combineTCR(contig_list,                         samples = c("P17B", "P17L", "P18B", "P18L",                                     "P19B","P19L", "P20B", "P20L"))  # Getting a sample of a Seurat object scRep_example <- get(data("scRep_example")) scRep_example <- combineExpression(combined,                                    scRep_example)  # Getting data frame output for Circlize circles <- getCirclize(scRep_example,                        group.by = "seurat_clusters")  # Multi-level grouping for hierarchical chord diagrams scRep_example$Patient <- substring(scRep_example$orig.ident, 1, 3) circles <- getCirclize(scRep_example,                        group.by = c("Patient", "seurat_clusters"))  # Get rich output with sector metadata result <- getCirclize(scRep_example,                       group.by = "seurat_clusters",                       include.metadata = TRUE)

title: Summarizes and Visualizes Gene Usage — percentGeneUsage
text: input.data product combineTCR(), combineBCR(), combineExpression(). chain TCR/BCR chain use. Accepted values: TRA, TRB, TRG, TRD, IGH, IGL (light chains) genes character vector specifying gene loci analyze. Can single gene e.g., "TRBV" "IGHJ" pair genes analysis (e.g., c("TRBV", "TRAV"), "TRBV", "TRBJ"). group.column header metadata lists group analysis (e.g., "sample", "treatment"). NULL, data analyzed list element active identity case single-cell objects. order.character vector defining desired order elements group.variable. Alternatively, use alphanumeric sort groups automatically. summary.fun Character string choosing summary statistic - "percent" (default), "proportion", "count". plot.type type plot return: "heatmap" (default paired loci, also available single loci), "barplot" (single loci). export.table TRUE, returns data frame matrix results instead plot. palette Colors use visualization - input exportTable Use export.table instead. hcl.pals. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot theme x.axis Gene segments separate x-axis, TRAV, TRBD, IGKJ. y.axis Variable separate y-axis, can categorical gene gene segments, TRAV, TRBD, IGKJ. plot type plot return - heatmap barplot. gene Vgene, Dgene Jgene

title: Summarizes and Visualizes Gene Usage — percentGeneUsage
text: ggplot object displaying heatmap bar plot gene usage. exportTable = TRUE, matrix data frame raw data returned.

title: Summarizes and Visualizes Gene Usage — percentGeneUsage
code: # Making combined contig data combined <- combineTCR(contig_list,                          samples = c("P17B", "P17L", "P18B", "P18L",                                      "P19B","P19L", "P20B", "P20L"))                                      # Visualize single gene (TRBV) usage as a heatmap, grouped by sample percentGeneUsage(combined,                  genes = "TRBV",                  group.by = "sample",                  plot.type = "heatmap",                  summary.fun = "percent")   # Visualize single gene (TRBV) usage as a barplot, grouped by sample percentGeneUsage(combined,                  genes = "TRBV",                  group.by = "sample",                  plot.type = "barplot",                  summary.fun = "count")   # Visualize paired gene (TRBV-TRBJ) usage as a heatmap percentGeneUsage(combined[1:2],                  genes = c("TRBV", "TRBJ"),                  group.by = "sample",                  plot.type = "heatmap",                  summary.fun = "proportion")   # Export the raw data table for single gene usage trbv_usage_table <- percentGeneUsage(combined,                                      genes = "TRBV",                                      group.by = "sample",                                      export.table = TRUE,                                      summary.fun = "count")  # Export the raw data table for paired gene usage trbv_trbj_usage_table <- percentGeneUsage(combined,                                           genes = c("TRBV", "TRBJ"),                                           group.by = "sample",                                           export.table = TRUE,                                           summary.fun = "percent")   # Visualize paired gene (TRAV-TRAJ) usage as a heatmap vizGenes(combined[1:2],          x.axis = "TRAV",          y.axis = "TRAJ",          group.by = "sample",          summary.fun = "count")   # Visualize cross-chain gene pairing (TRBV-TRAV) vizGenes(combined[1:2],          x.axis = "TRBV",          y.axis = "TRAV",          group.by = "sample",          summary.fun = "percent")    # Quantify and visualize TRA V-gene usage as a heatmap percentGenes(combined,              chain = "TRA",              gene = "Vgene",              group.by = "sample",              summary.fun = "percent")   # Quantify TRA J-gene usage and export the table traj_usage_table <- percentGenes(combined,                                  chain = "TRA",                                  gene = "Jgene",                                  group.by = "sample",                                  export.table = TRUE,                                  summary.fun = "count")  # Quantify and visualize TRB V-J gene pairings as a heatmap percentVJ(combined[1:2],           chain = "TRB",           group.by = "sample",           summary.fun = "percent")   # 2. Quantify TRA V-J gene pairings and export the table trav_traj_table <- percentVJ(combined,                              chain = "TRA",                              group.by = "sample",                              export.table = TRUE,                              summary.fun = "proportion")

what: Visualize Clonal Relationships as a Chord Diagram — vizCirclize
title: Visualize Clonal Relationships as a Chord Diagram — vizCirclize
text: function creates chord diagram visualization shared clones groups using circlize package. provides convenient wrapper around getCirclize() handles circlize plotting code automatically.

title: Visualize Clonal Relationships as a Chord Diagram — vizCirclize
code: vizCirclize(   sc.data,   clone.call = NULL,   group.by = NULL,   method = c("unique", "jaccard", "overlap"),   proportion = FALSE,   directional = FALSE,   self.link = 1,   include.self = TRUE,   transparency = 0.5,   link.visible = TRUE,   annotate.sectors = TRUE,   min.shared = 0,   palette = "inferno",   sector.colors = NULL,   export.table = FALSE )

title: Visualize Clonal Relationships as a Chord Diagram — vizCirclize
text: sc.data single-cell object combineExpression(). clone.call Defines clonal sequence grouping. Accepted values : gene (VDJC genes), nt (CDR3 nucleotide sequence), aa (CDR3 amino acid sequence), strict (VDJC + nt). custom column header can also used. group.column header (vector column headers hierarchical grouping) metadata group analysis . method method calculating link values: "unique" (default) counts unique shared clones, "jaccard" calculates Jaccard similarity, "overlap" calculates overlap coefficient. proportion Calculate relationship unique clones (FALSE, default) normalized proportion (TRUE). directional TRUE, show directional arrows chords. Default FALSE. self.link handle self-links. 1 = show loops, 2 = show parallel lines. Default 1. include.self Include self-links (clones within single group). Default TRUE. transparency Transparency chord links (0-1). Default 0.5. link.visible FALSE, hide chord links show sectors. annotate.sectors TRUE (default), display sector names. min.shared Minimum number shared clones include link (default 0). palette Colors use sectors - input hcl.pals. sector.colors Named vector colors specific sectors. Overrides palette. export.table TRUE, returns data instead plotting.

title: Visualize Clonal Relationships as a Chord Diagram — vizCirclize
text: Invisibly returns circlize data list. circlize installed export.table = TRUE, returns data used plotting.

title: Visualize Clonal Relationships as a Chord Diagram — vizCirclize

title: Visualize Clonal Relationships as a Chord Diagram — vizCirclize
text: Nick Borcherding

title: Visualize Clonal Relationships as a Chord Diagram — vizCirclize
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Getting the combined contigs combined <- combineTCR(contig_list,                         samples = c("P17B", "P17L", "P18B", "P18L",                                     "P19B","P19L", "P20B", "P20L"))  # Getting a sample of a Seurat object scRep_example <- get(data("scRep_example")) scRep_example <- combineExpression(combined,                                    scRep_example)  # Simple chord diagram vizCirclize(scRep_example, group.by = "seurat_clusters")  # Directional chord diagram with arrows vizCirclize(scRep_example,             group.by = "seurat_clusters",             directional = TRUE)  # Multi-level grouping scRep_example$Patient <- substring(scRep_example$orig.ident, 1, 3) vizCirclize(scRep_example,             group.by = c("Patient", "seurat_clusters")) } # }

title: scRepertoire VERSION 2.7.2
text: New clonalBin() function bin clones frequency proportion without requiring single-cell object. Adds clonalFrequency, clonalProportion, cloneSize columns output combineTCR(), combineBCR(), combineExpression(). Supports custom bin thresholds, optional grouping metadata variable, chain filtering. New vizCirclize() function quick chord diagram visualization without manual circlize code. Supports directional arrows, custom colors, sector annotations. Multi-level hierarchical grouping via group.accepting vector columns New method parameter: "unique", "abundance", "jaccard", "overlap" New symmetric parameter directional flow analysis New include.metadata parameter returning sector statistics New filtering options: min.shared, top.links, filter.sectors Built-color palette generation palette parameter New top.clones, min.freq, highlight.clones, highlight.color parameters Visual customization: stratum.width, flow.alpha, show.labels, label.size New order.strata parameter controlling level ordering within stratum Enhanced export.table output now includes freq, prop, rank columns combineBCR() defaults clustering call “IGH” instead “”

title: scRepertoire VERSION 2.7.2
text: Soft-deprecated camelCase arguments across exported functions favor dot.notation (e.g., cloneCall clone.call, exportTable export.table, cloneSize clone.size, filterNA filter.na, addLabel add.label, clonalSplit clonal.split). deprecated arguments continue work deprecation warning version 3.0.0.

title: scRepertoire VERSION 2.7.2
text: Fixed combineExpression() failing “undefined columns selected” input data already contained clonalFrequency/clonalProportion columns prior clonalBin() call.

title: scRepertoire VERSION 2.6.2

title: scRepertoire VERSION 2.5.1
text: Introduced pairwise calculations StartracDiversity(). Internal function conversion clonalSizeDistribution() - removed cubature, truncdist, VGAM dependencies. Increased speed clonalSizeDistribution().

title: scRepertoire VERSION 2.5.0
text: Updated improved code loadContigs(). Consolidated support discrete AIRR formats umbrella AIRR. Added "tcrpheno" "immunarch" exportClones(). Converted exportClones() base R reduce dependencies. Added dandelionR tcrpheno vignettes pkgdown site. percentAA() refactored minimize dependencies use immApex calculateFrequency(). positionalEntropy() refactored minimize dependencies use immApex calculateEntropy(). clonalDiversity() refactored performance - now calculates single diversity metric time includes new estimators like "gini", "d50", supports hill numbers. percentKmer() refactored use immApex calculateMotif() aa nt sequences. longer calculates possible motifs, motifs present. clonalCluster() now allows dual-chain clustering, V/J filtering, normalized straight edit distance calculations, return clusters, igraph objects, adjacency matrix. combineBCR() offers single/dual chain clustering, aa nt sequences, adaptive filtering V J genes, normalized straight edit distance calculations. percentGeneUsage() now underlying function percentGenes(), percentVJ(), vizGenes() allows percent, proportion, raw count quantification. Added common theme (internal .themeRepertoire()) plots allow users pass arguments .

title: scRepertoire VERSION 2.5.0
text: clonalCompare() issue plotting 0 row data frame now errors message. clonalScatter() group./axes call now works non-single-cell objects. Fixed issue NULL “none” group.combineExpression(). Allowing multi groupings via x.axis group.clonalDiversity().

text: Fixed issue single chain output clonalLength(). Removed unnecessary code remnant clonalLength(). Allow one sample plotted percentVJ(). Fixed issue positionalProperty() exportTable. Fixed issue loadContigs() edge case TRUST4 data 1 row. Converted documentation use markdown (roxygen2md). Imported lifecycle, purrr, withr. Fixed issue clonalCluster() exportGraph = TRUE. Improved performance combineBCR() constant factor C++. Restructured functions exportTable plotting.

title: scRepertoire VERSION 2.0.4
text: getCirclize() refactored prevent assumptions added include.self argument. Added .count.clones() internal function getCirclize() clonalNetwork(). Added order.parameter visualizations specifically call order plotting using vector "alphanumeric". Fixed issue clonalLength() NA handling. clonalCompare() now retains original clonal info using relabel.clones. Added Dandelion support loadContigs() testthat. Fixed issue positionalProperty() assumption clones 20 amino acids. Fixed issue positionalProperty() removing non-amino acids. Fixed IGH/K/L mistaking gene issue vizGenes(). Added error message NULL results clonalCluster() export.graph = TRUE. Fixed issue “full.clones” missing combineExpression() using 1 chain.

title: scRepertoire VERSION 2.0.3
text: Modified support Omniscope format allow dual chains. Added ParseBio support loadContigs() testthat. Added support productive variable loadContigs() BD, Omniscope, Immcantation formats. Replaced numerical indexing name indexing loadContigs(). combineBCR() combineTCR() now allow unproductive contig inclusions new filterNonproductive parameter. combineBCR() now prompt user samples included instead erroring. Added base threshold length internal .lvCompare(). Ensured internal .lvCompare() looks first set sequences multi-sequence chain. Fixed bug exporting graph clonalCluster(). Fixed conflict functions igraph dplyr packages.

title: scRepertoire VERSION 2.0.0 (2024-01-10)
text: Added percentAA(), percentGenes(), percentVJ(), percentKmer(), exportClones(), positionalEntropy(), positionalProperty(). Renamed functions: compareClonotypes clonalCompare(), clonotypeSizeDistribution clonalSizeDistribution(), scatterClonotypes clonalScatter(), quantContig clonalQuant(), highlightClonotypes highlightClones(), lengthContigs clonalLength(), occupiedscRepertoire clonalOccupy(), abundanceContig clonalAbundance(), alluvialClonotypes alluvialClones(). Added features clonalCompare() allow highlighting sequences relabeling clonotypes.

title: scRepertoire VERSION 2.0.0 (2024-01-10)
text: Removed internal .quiet() function. .theCall() now allows custom header/variable checks colnames. Replaced data arguments descriptive: df input.data, dir input, sc sc.data. Deep clean documentation increased consistency explainability. StartracDiversity() metric re-implemented remove startrac-class object intermediary. Implemented powerTCR locally reduce dependencies continue support. Universalized underlying function language intermediate variables. License change MIT. group.split.consolidated single group.parameter. Added support Immcantation pipeline, .json, Omniscope, MiXCR formats loadContigs(). Made GitHub.io website support/vignettes/FAQ. Added testthat exported internal functions. Fixed issue clonalQuant() instance scale = FALSE group.set. clonalDiversity() longer automatically orders samples. Removed order parameter clonalQuant(), clonalLength(), clonalAbundance(). x.axis parameter clonalDiversity() separated group.parameter. Filtering chains eliminate non-matching chains.

Added support BD loadContigs(). loadContigs() TRUST4 parsing allows NA values chain. combineExpression() group.= NULL now collapse whole list.

title: scRepertoire VERSION 1.7.0
text: Rebumping version change new release Bioconductor. Added mean call heatmap vizGenes(). filteringMulti combineTCR now checks remove list elements 0 cells. Removed top_n() call (deprecated), using slice_max() without ties. Added arrange() call parseTCR() organize chains. Corrected gd flip combine functions. Removed viridis call clonalNetwork() leading errors. Matched syntax strict clonotype combineBCR(). Added group.variable applicable visualizations. Added return.boots clonalDiversity() allow export bootstrapped values.

title: scRepertoire VERSION 1.5.4
text: Modified grabMeta() internal function longer assume active identity clusters. checkBlanks() now checks blank detected trying remove . clonalNetwork() automatically resulted default error message - bug now removed. clonalBias() now adds z-score bias matrix exported. exportTable parameter now fixed.

title: scRepertoire VERSION 1.5.3
text: Added loadContigs() non-10X formatted single-cell data. Removed combineTRUST4, superseded loadContigs(). combineTCR() now allows > 3 recovered TCRs per barcode. Re-added filtering steps combineTCR(), detect data 10X automatically remove nonproductive multi chains. Updated parseTCR() include evaluation gamma/delta chains.

title: scRepertoire VERSION 1.5.2
text: highlightClones() now returns specific clones instead clonotype 1, etc. clonalCompare() numbers parameter now group-wide numbers overall top X numbers. Fixed issue clonalDiversity() caused errors group.parameter used. Modified parseBCR() reduce complexity assume lambda >> kappa. Fixed clonalCluster() function broken Seurat Objects. checkContigs now ensures data frames “” converted NAs. Modified makeGenes() internal function changing na.omit str_replace_na() separating BCR calls chain prevent combination errors.

Added clonalNetwork() function. Modified diversity visualization remove outliers place graphs single line. Modified clonalOverlay() use new internal getCoord() function like clonalNetwork(). Added threshold parameter clonalSizeDistribution(). Added support single-cell objects clonalCluster().

title: scRepertoire VERSION 1.3.4
text: Modified clonalCluster() combineBCR() speed comparison use less memory. filteringMulti now isolates top contig chain, barcodes chains > 2, isolates top expressing chains. Modified makeGenes() internal function use str_c(). Added threshold parameter combineTRUST4 B cell manipulation. Changed combineTCR function prevent cell type mix . vizGenes() can now look component genes receptor separate parameter replaced y.axis. Added clonalBias() function inter-cluster comparison. Fixed clonalCluster() combineBCR() assumption unrelated clones.

title: scRepertoire VERSION 1.3.3
text: combineBCR() auto naming function updated actually name list elements. Added createHTOContigList() function create contig list multiplexed experiments. Fixed issue groupBy variable. Added Inverse Pielou metric diversity call. Added include.na split.clonalOccupy() changed labeling depending frequency vs proportion. Added support single-cell objects visualizations, list organizing single-cell object can called using split.variable. group groupBy parameters now group..

title: scRepertoire VERSION 1.3.2
text: Added dot.size parameter clonalScatter(). filteringMulti now subsets clonotypes contigs >= 2, prevent 2 chains. Changed coldata added SCE objects using merge instead union. Can now add BCR TCR simultaneously making large list. Scatter plotting code allows user select dot.size variable x y axis. Removed regressClonotype function - many dependencies required. Added chain option visualizations combineExpression() allow users facilitate single chains - removed chain option combineTCR/combineBCR/combineTRUST4. Added NA filter combineTCR/combineBCR/combineTRUST4 cell barcodes NA values. Added NA filter expression2List() cells NA clonotypes. Updated vizGenes() order genes automatically highest lowest variance. Updated vizGenes() pull correct genes based selection. Updated parse method V/J/D placement TRB/Heavy chains. Simplified clonalDiversity() allow options organizing plot box plots. combineExpression() adds groupBy variable Frequency, allowing multiple calculations saved meta data.
